title: CFPE Sensitivity Expansion: ±30% Prior Variation, Edge Removal, Impact Weight Sweep
methodology: Systematic perturbation of all model parameters to identify fragile assumptions, high-leverage edges, and anti-fragility floor. Extends the original ±20% sensitivity to ±30% with edge-removal analysis and impact-weight sweeping.

base_cascade.post_evidence_synthesis_note: EV revised from original 0.524 to 0.135 after evidence synthesis uncovered FCI contradiction and 55× threshold gap

prior_sensitivity_plus_minus_30.description: Each Era 1 prior is individually perturbed by ±30%, with remaining probability mass redistributed proportionally to other states. Cascade EV recomputed for each perturbation.

perturbations.interpretation: E1_Opt has direct proportional impact on the all-optimistic path (±30% prior → ±30% path probability). Moderately sensitive — a 30% error in this prior produces a ~15% error in cascade EV.

decreased_30.note: Paradoxical: decreasing Baseline INCREASES all-Opt probability because probability mass shifts to Optimistic

perturbations.interpretation: E1_Baseline is the MOST sensitive parameter. Increasing it by 30% (from 0.60 to 0.78) reduces cascade EV by 20%. This is because Baseline crowds out Optimistic — and the cascade needs Optimistic Era 1 to reach Optimistic Eras 2-4. The model is highly sensitive to how much probability mass is assigned to 'nothing interesting happens.'
E1_Pess is the LEAST sensitive parameter — the pessimistic path has low throughput to Eras 2-4 because P(E2_Opt|E1_Pess) = 0.05 (very low). The model is robust to errors in the pessimistic prior.

prior_tornado_summary.critical_finding: The cascade EV is driven primarily by E1_Baseline, not by the tail events. The model's uncertainty about the future is dominated by uncertainty about whether the continuity baseline holds — the paradigm-shift scenarios are secondary. This is consistent with the Base Rate Neglect check from deep-research Stage 2: the probability of ANY paradigm shift should be bounded by historical base rates (~0.05-0.10 per decade).

conditional_probability_sensitivity.description: Each conditional probability in the 3 matrices (27 total entries) is perturbed by ±0.15 (bounded to [0,1]). Cascade EV recomputed. Top-5 most impactful edges are reported.

most_impactful_edges.label: Leverage Node: Era 2→3 Golden Key
most_impactful_edges.importance: CRITICAL — single most impactful conditional edge. The Eras 2→3 optimistic link is the leverage node: if bio-spintronics succeeds, hydrodynamic foundations become highly probable (85%). Varying this edge by ±15% changes the all-optimistic path by ±17.6%.

most_impactful_edges.label: Era 1→2 Optimistic Bridge
most_impactful_edges.importance: CRITICAL — second most impactful edge. Controls whether Era 1 FCI success translates to Era 2 bio-spintronics. Physical interpretation: if FCI braiding works but can't be adapted to room-temperature substrates, the cascade breaks here.

most_impactful_edges.label: Era 3→4 Optimistic Link
most_impactful_edges.importance: HIGH — third most impactful. The large percentage delta reflects the low base value (0.40). This edge represents the probability that hydrodynamic foundations (Era 3) will lead to geometric constant derivation (Era 4) — the most speculative link in the cascade.

most_impactful_edges.label: Choke Node: Era 3→4 Pessimistic Lock-In
most_impactful_edges.importance: HIGH — the choke node. If Era 3 hits Pessimistic, there's an 80% chance of staying pessimistic through Era 4. Reducing this to 65% (e.g., via institutional incentives) could improve worst-case EV by ~3%.

most_impactful_edges.label: Era 1→2 Pessimistic Lock-In
most_impactful_edges.importance: MODERATE — controls how strongly Era 1 failure locks in Era 2 failure. Improving recovery from Era 1 failure has modest impact on total EV because Era 1 pessimistic probability is only 0.15.

edge_removal_analysis.description: Each dependency edge is removed by setting the conditional probability equal to the marginal probability (i.e., making the two eras independent). This measures how much information each edge contributes to the cascade.

removals.interpretation: Removing the leverage node EDGE halves the all-optimistic path probability. This demonstrates that the cascade's most distinctive prediction (all-paradigm-shift path) depends critically on this single conditional — if bio-spintronics does NOT make hydrodynamic foundations more likely than the base rate, the entire cascade collapses to near-zero.

Removing the choke node edge reduces the all-pessimistic path by 28%. The cascade's pessimistic scenario is driven by the strong lock-in effect from Era 3 to Era 4 — without it, the pessimistic path is 28% less probable.

Similar to the leverage node: the Era 1→2 optimistic link carries >50% of the cascade's information. If FCI success does NOT imply bio-spintronics success, the paradigm-shift cascade probability drops by half.
The Era 3→4 link is less impactful than the earlier links because P(E4_Opt) = 0.310 is closer to P(E4_Opt|E3_Opt) = 0.40 — the conditional doesn't deviate as far from the marginal.

aggregate_removal.interpretation: If ALL dependency edges are removed (all eras become independent), the all-paradigm-shift path probability drops to 1.0% — an 83% reduction from the base cascade. This confirms that the cascade's probabilities are almost entirely driven by the conditional dependencies, not the marginal base rates. The model is a CASCADE model, not a collection of independent forecasts.

impact_weight_sweep.description: The original CFPE model used a multi-dimensional impact score: I = w_s×S + w_t×T + w_c×C + w_e×E where S=scientific, T=technological, C=societal, E=economic. We sweep each weight from 0 to 1 and recompute the Pareto-optimal portfolio.

weight_configurations.rationale: Maximum scientific impact without regard for technological feasibility
Maximum technological impact — FCI is the most hardware-tangible candidate
Maximum societal/philosophical impact — rewriting quantum foundations has the broadest cultural implications
Maximum economic impact — gate-based QC has the largest existing commercial ecosystem
Balanced portfolio — bio-spintronics scores well across all dimensions
Societal-dominant weighting — paradigm shift in worldview prioritized

impact_weight_sweep.robustness: The top candidate is NOT robust to weight variation — it changes from Era 3 (science) to Era 1 (tech) to Era 3 again (societal) depending on which dimension dominates. This suggests the CFPE forecast does NOT identify a single 'best' research direction — the optimal choice depends crucially on value judgments about which impact dimension matters most.

pareto_frontier.description: Candidates on the Pareto frontier (not dominated by any other candidate on any dimension):
candidates: Era 1: FCI Braiding (dominates on tech/economic, weak on science/societal → picks up commodity QC applications)
Era 2: Bio-Spintronics (balanced across all dimensions, no single dimension dominates)
Era 3: Hydrodynamic Foundations (dominates on science/societal, weak on tech/economic → pure theory)
pareto_frontier.note: Era 4: Adelic Physics is dominated on ALL dimensions by Era 3 — it offers lower probability, lower impact, and no unique dimensional advantage.

anti_fragility_analysis.description: The anti-fragility floor is the minimum cascade EV achievable under any single-point failure. We compute it by setting each era's optimistic probability to its minimum plausible value.

worst_case_scenarios.recovery: Divert to NV-diamond centers (E2-P3 wildcard) — preserves Era 2+ probability
Pivot to classical thermodynamic computing (end of bio-spintronics pipeline)
Pivot to CSL objective collapse (E3-P3 wildcard) — salvage foundations program
Era 4 is terminal — no downstream dependency, failure is contained

anti_fragility_analysis.interpretation: The CFPE cascade has a single point of failure at Era 2: if room-temperature coherence proves impossible (<1% probability of E2_Opt), the cascade EV drops to 3.8% — a 72% reduction. The counterfactual pivot (NV centers, E2-P3 wildcard) was identified in the pathway matrix but was NOT incorporated into the Bayesian model's conditional matrices. RECOMMENDATION: add NV-center pathway as an explicit alternative to the bio-spintronics pathway with its own conditional matrix to improve anti-fragility.
anti_fragility_analysis.flag: [FRAGILE: single point of failure at Era 2. Anti-fragility floor is 28.1% of base EV — well below the 50% threshold.]

sensitivity_summary.most_impactful_edge: P(E3_Opt|E2_Opt) — the leverage node (±15% → ±3.1% cascade EV delta)
sensitivity_summary.most_impactful_removal: Removing ALL conditional dependencies reduces all-opt path by 83%
sensitivity_summary.anti_fragility_floor: 0.038 (28.1% of base EV) — FRAGILE flag triggered
sensitivity_summary.weight_sensitivity: Top candidate changes with impact weights — no single 'best' research direction

hallve_all_priors_check.description: MANDATORY check from deep-research Stage 5: multiply all candidate priors by 0.50, recompute optimal portfolio.

result.portfolio_change: Portfolio composition unchanged (all probabilities halved proportionally), but the cascade EV drops below the 10% threshold for actionable forecasting. Flag: [PRIOR-SENSITIVE: halving priors puts EV below actionable threshold — expert elicitation recommended]

hallve_all_priors_check.flag: [PRIOR-SENSITIVE] — the portfolio is robust to proportional prior changes (composition stable) but the absolute EV is sensitive (drops below actionable threshold).
